urgent_sounds.description: A rhythmic sequence of three mechanical 'Ding-Dong' chimes. Each chime features a high-frequency percussive attack (approx. 800-1000Hz) followed by a resonant lower-tone resolution. The audio signature is characterized by natural metallic decay and a consistent 1.5-second interval between chime events, indicating a physical solenoid-driven striker mechanism rather than a digital synthesized tone.

A clear human vocalization of a proper noun, characterized by a distinct two-to-three syllable cadence. The audio signature features a natural speech frequency range (typically 85Hz to 255Hz) with a gentle melodic rise or fall on the final syllable. It is marked by a sharp phonetic onset and a soft breathy decay, set against a low-noise background to emphasize vocal clarity and person-to-person communication intent.
